bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility by proposing my top value on every issue. The opponent opened with the lowest-utility value for me on all issues, so there is not yet evidence of concession or a mutually acceptable tradeoff.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.973) because it is still extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.02), so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has moved slightly from all valueD to improving issueA to valueC, which raises my utility from 0.20 to about 0.308, but their offers remain far from acceptable; I will make a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has improved from all valueD to concessions on issueA and issueB, but their offers are still low utility for me, so I will hold firm on the high-importance issues and only move issueD further.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers are improving for me from about 0.20 to 0.493, suggesting they are conceding, and I will continue conceding first on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers are clearly improving for me, especially on issueA, but they have held issueC and issueD at valueD, so I concede more on those while protecting my high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.10, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially on issueA and issueB, while they continue holding issueC and issueD low; with no messages to qualify this, I will make a limited concession on issueB while keeping my strongest positions on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.87 because it is still early at t=0.12, so I should not concede down to the opponent’s latest offer yet. The opponent has been steadily conceding on my most important issues, now offering issueA and issueB at valueA, while holding issueC and issueD at valueD, so I concede fully on low-weight issueD and partially on issueC while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.81 while making a meaningful concession on issueC, since the opponent has repeatedly held issueC and issueD at valueD while conceding issueA and issueB toward my preferred values. At t=0.14 it is still early, so I should hold firm on the more important issueA and issueB, but avoid repeating my exact previous bid by moving issueC from valueB to valueC.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility while making a modest concession from my high-utility bids, since time is still only 0.16. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB but remains tough on issueC and issueD, so I will hold firm on the most important issueA while offering movement on lower/mid-weight issues to test for acceptance.

I am targeting a utility around 0.81, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to rush down to the opponent’s latest bid. The opponent has steadily conceded toward my preferred values, especially on issueA and issueB, while consistently holding issueD at valueD, so I concede issueD and partly concede issueC while holding firm on higher-weight issueA and issueB.

At t=0.20 it is still early enough to target a relatively high utility bid, but I should make a real concession from my last offer rather than hold completely firm. The opponent has conceded a lot on issueA and issueB, while consistently pushing issueC/issueD lower for me, so I will keep my strongest issueA at valueA, concede somewhat on issueB and issueD, and avoid dropping issueC all the way to their recent valueC demand.

At t=0.22, there is still enough time to target a relatively high-utility bid around 0.81 while making concessions on the lower-weight issues C and D. The opponent has repeatedly conceded on issueA and issueB, even offering issueA:valueA and issueB:valueA before, while holding issueD at valueD, so I will keep my strongest positions on A/B and concede D fully with a moderate concession on C.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still preserving my most important issue, issueA. Time is still relatively early at t=0.24, so I should not concede issueA yet, but the opponent has signaled willingness to offer issueA:valueA with issueB:valueB and issueC:valueC before, making this a plausible compromise. There are no opponent messages, and all offers so far use valid domain issues and values.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.76, which is still strong for me at t=0.26 while making concessions mainly on lower-weight issueC and issueD. The opponent previously offered issueA: valueA and issueB: valueA together, but has consistently kept issueD at valueD and often pushed issueC low, so this bid uses that signal without conceding my most important issues.

At t=0.28 there is still time, so I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid while avoiding repeating my immediately previous offer. The opponent has shown willingness to give valueA on issueA and sometimes issueB, but continues to push issueD down, so I concede issueD while holding firmer on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79 because we are still only at t=0.30, so I should concede gradually but not drop to the opponent’s latest low-value offer yet. The opponent has moved substantially on issueA and issueB but continues to hold issueC and issueD low, so I will keep my most important issueA at valueA, concede issueD fully, and make moderate concessions on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.814 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and B while conceding issueD fully and issueC partially. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I do not need to drop to the opponent’s best prior offer yet; their offers show they can accept valueA on issueA and sometimes issueB, while they consistently push for valueD on issueD. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

At t=0.34 I am still targeting a fairly strong utility around 0.785, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueD and moderately on issueB/issueC while holding issueA at my top value. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially from the start and they have repeatedly signaled willingness to give valueA on issueA or issueB, but they seem to push hard for worse values on issueC and issueD. Since my previous bid was issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueD, I avoid repeating it and offer a different compromise.

I am targeting a utility around 0.81 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and B while conceding on lower-weight issueD and partially on issueC. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I do not need to drop all the way to the opponent’s latest offer, though their bids show movement toward my preferred values on issueA and issueB while they remain firm on issueD. Since their latest valid offer was worth about 0.679 to me, this counter keeps pressure while offering a realistic middle step.

At t=0.38, I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.78 rather than conceding to the opponent’s weaker recent offer. The opponent has consistently kept issueD at valueD and has often pushed issueC low, so I concede on the low-weight issueD and partially on issueB/C while holding issueA at my top value.

I am targeting a utility around 0.81, keeping my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB at valueA while conceding on lower-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.40 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest low-utility offer; their history shows they have previously offered valueA on issueA/issueB and valueC on issueC, while consistently holding issueD at valueD, so I concede issueD fully.

I am targeting a utility around 0.81, keeping the high-weight issues A and B at my best values while conceding issueD fully and offering a moderate compromise on issueC. At t=0.42 there is still time, so I should not immediately accept the opponent’s latest 0.76-equivalent bid, but their movement to issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA suggests those terms may now be acceptable. With no messages to weigh, I rely on the offer pattern: they are holding issueC/issueD low for me, so I concede only the lowest-weight issue fully and ask for issueC=valueC.

At t=0.44, I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.76 rather than conceding too far, since there is still time before the deadline. The opponent has already offered {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD} at t=0.42, so this bid uses a package they have signaled as possible while preserving my two highest-weight issues.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.77, which is a meaningful concession from my ideal while still preserving my highest-weight issueA. The opponent has already offered this exact combination earlier, suggesting it may be acceptable to them; at t=0.46 there is pressure to move toward signaled feasible bids, but not enough to concede below prior opponent-compatible utility.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.76 rather than conceding further, since there is still about half the time left. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueC and issueD down to valueD, but has at least once offered issueA: valueA and issueB: valueA, so I will accept concessions on the lower-weight issues while holding the two most important issues at valueA. There are no messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.73, conceding on issueD and partially on issueB/issueC while holding issueA at my top value because it has the highest weight. The opponent has repeatedly signaled willingness to give issueA: valueA and has offered combinations close to this, while still tending to hold issueD at valueD; with no messages, I rely only on the offer pattern.

At t=0.52 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility while prioritizing agreement on terms the opponent has already signaled willingness to offer. The opponent has repeatedly kept issueC and issueD low for me but has conceded on the higher-weight issueA and issueB at times, including offering issueA:valueA and issueB:valueA with issueC/issueD at valueD; with no messages, I rely only on the offer pattern. This bid holds firm on my most important issues while conceding the lower-weight issues to values the opponent has consistently pushed.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.73 by holding firm on high-weight issueA while conceding issueD fully and compromising on issueB/issueC. The opponent has repeatedly kept issueD at valueD and recently offered issueA: valueA with issueB: valueB and issueC: valueD, so I am asking for a modest improvement on issueC without repeating my previous exact bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, which is still strong for me while making meaningful concessions on the lower-weight issues C and D. At t=0.56 we are in the mid-game, so I should move toward bids the opponent has already signaled as acceptable; they have previously offered this exact valid bid more than once, and there are no messages to weigh against the offer history.

I am targeting a utility around 0.733, which is a moderate concession from my best outcomes while still protecting the high-weight issueA. At t=0.58 there is some time pressure, but not enough to drop all the way to the opponent’s latest lower-utility offer; importantly, the opponent has previously offered this exact combination, so it is a plausible settlement point.

I am targeting the opponent's latest bid, which gives me about 0.76 utility: strong on my two most important issues, while conceding issueC and issueD. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent has repeatedly signaled willingness to give issueA and issueB while holding firm on the lower-weight issues, so matching their current acceptable bid is prudent.
